name: Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop
name_original: Santiago de Compostela - vuelta del Río Ulla por Estrada
description: This difficult 40.8-mile road cycling route near Santiago de Compostela features 4073 feet of elevation gain and takes about 3.5 hours.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around A Estrada offer diverse landscapes and varied terrain, making it a suitable region for cyclists of different skill levels. The area is characterized by a mix of climbs and flatter sections, often alongside rivers and towards natural landmarks. Cyclists can explore routes that incorporate historical sites and cultural points of interest within the municipality of A Estrada. Best road cycling routes around A Estrada The most popular road cycling route is Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop, a 40.8 miles (65.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and scenic river views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cimadavila Spring – Pontillón do Castro Reservoir loop from A Estrada, a difficult 49.4 miles (79.5 km) path. This route includes substantial climbing and passes by a reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Pontevea Medieval Bridge – Padrón Bridge loop from A Estrada, a 34.0 miles (54.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Road cycling around A Estrada is defined by varied terrain, river valleys, and opportunities to visit historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in A Estrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore A Estrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around A Estrada?
A: A Estrada offers a diverse mix of terrains for road cycling. You'll find routes with significant climbs, as well as flatter sections, often winding alongside rivers. The landscape provides varied challenges and scenic views, catering to different preferences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in A Estrada?
A: While many routes in A Estrada are rated moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. This allows beginners to enjoy the region's scenery at a comfortable pace.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974485276/pico-sacro-and-ulla-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over 40.8 miles. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40367060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimadavila Spring – Pontillón do Castro Reservoir loop from A Estrada&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 49.4 miles with substantial climbing.

Q: How long do road cycling routes around A Estrada typically take to complete?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in A Estrada varies depending on the distance and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15019548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontevea Medieval Bridge – Padrón Bridge loop from A Estrada&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 34.0-mile route, typically takes around 2 hours 52 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes can take upwards of 3.5 hours.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with visiting historical sites in A Estrada?
A: Absolutely. Many road cycling routes in A Estrada allow you to incorporate visits to historical and cultural points of interest. You can cycle past sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/570232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Mariña de Carracedo Church&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Escravitude&lt;/a&gt;, enriching your ride with cultural exploration.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks to see while cycling?
A: Yes, the region around A Estrada is rich in natural beauty. Cyclists can enjoy routes that lead to or pass by impressive natural landmarks. For example, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3855179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Toxa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1536265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raxoi Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque stops along your journey.

Q: Are there routes that pass by water features like reservoirs or waterfalls?
A: Several routes in A Estrada feature beautiful water elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40367060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimadavila Spring – Pontillón do Castro Reservoir loop from A Estrada&lt;/a&gt; passes by a reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Umia Reservoir – Caldas de Reis loop from A Estrada&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views of water bodies. Additionally, the region is home to several waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6112687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valga Mills Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into your rides.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in A Estrada?
A: The road cycling experience in A Estrada is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the opportunities to explore river valleys, and the chance to visit historical sites, making for a well-rounded and engaging ride.

Q: Are there any loop routes available for road cycling in A Estrada?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around A Estrada are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974485276/pico-sacro-and-ulla-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20510288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coffee and Pastries – Casa Samuel loop from A Estrada&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your ride.

Q: Can I find routes that offer a mix of cycling and local exploration, perhaps with cafes or points of interest?
A: Certainly. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20510288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coffee and Pastries – Casa Samuel loop from A Estrada&lt;/a&gt; are designed to combine cycling with opportunities to experience local culture, suggesting stops at establishments like cafes. The region's proximity to towns like Caldas de Reis also means you can find places to refresh and explore.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in A Estrada?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the general climate of Galicia suggests that spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the heat of summer and the colder, wetter conditions of winter. These seasons typically provide ideal conditions for enjoying the varied terrain and natural beauty.
tips: An isolated mountain peak, giving it a unique point of reference in the surrounding landscape. The top of Pico Sacro is the first place from where you can see the towers of the church of Santiago de Compostela

The exact date of the current bridge is not known, even though historians place its construction around the 14th-15th centuries, replacing or modifying an earlier bridge, possibly Roman, related to the 19th road of the Antonine itinerary, which connected Braga with Lugo and crossed the "Pons Vellegia" (Pontevea) coming from Aquis Celenis (Cuntis).

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a hard road ride and requires very good fitness. With over 1,240 meters of elevation gain across 65.6 kilometers, it presents a significant challenge for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily a road ride, featuring mostly well-paved surfaces. However, given its 'hard' classification and substantial elevation gain, expect demanding sections and significant climbs. While generally good for road bikes, some challenging segments may require extra effort.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Santiago de Compostela - return of the Río Ulla by Estrada route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Santiago de Compostela or near Estrada, depending on your preferred starting point. Look for public parking areas in these towns. Specific parking details for cyclists are not provided, so general town parking would be your best option.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the starting point with a bike?
faqs.answer: Public transport options in Galicia, such as buses and trains, may allow bikes, but policies vary. It's advisable to check with local transport providers in Santiago de Compostela or Estrada regarding their specific rules and availability for bike transport before planning your journey.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the impressive Pico Sacro, a prominent summit, and the historic Pontevea Medieval Bridge. The connection to the Río Ulla also suggests picturesque river views and scenic Galician countryside.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons offer milder temperatures and less rainfall, making the challenging climbs more enjoyable. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring more rain and cooler conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route, especially within Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads in Galicia, including this route which passes through the Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is the Santiago de Compostela - return of the Río Ulla by Estrada route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it is not typically considered 'dog-friendly' in the same way a hiking trail might be. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, cycling with a dog on a leash on a challenging road route like this is not recommended for safety reasons for both the cyclist and the dog.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of several other paths, including the Caminho da Geira e dos Arrieiros, Camiño Xabrés (Oeste), and the Sendeiro Rural de Galicia, among others. These intersections are typically short segments.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the route?
faqs.answer: While specific cafes or water points are not detailed for this exact route, cycling in the region around Santiago de Compostela and Estrada often means passing through small towns or villages where you can find cafes, bars, or shops to refuel and rehydrate. It's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's difficulty.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging road ride like this?
faqs.answer: For this demanding road ride, you should bring a well-maintained racebike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, sufficient water and nutrition, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), navigation tools (like a GPS device or komoot app), and a fully charged phone. Given the elevation, layers of clothing are also recommended.
